title: Chamber of Guf Revisited
wikitext:
[]: "Chamber of Guf" Revisited....             Quote Post by V » Sun Mar 16, 2008 2:14 pm Two questions, I hope this isn't an exact retread of earlier discussion: First, Ritsuko's reference to the "Chamber of Guf was empty" refers to the fact that they couldn't get more souls....meaning what exactly? Interpretation 1 is "There are no more souls, the End Times are drawing nigh" But I'm starting to lean towards a second interpretation that: "In the NGEverse, clones do not have souls, be they "Rei Ayanami" or "Evangelions". The "Chamber of Guf is empty" is just a "fancy way" of saying "we can't make souls on our own from scratch with clones, and we couldn't get new ones from Adam because Adam was dormant. So we jerry-rigged the system by either using transfered human souls, or fractions of human souls we found" etc. What was everyone else's position? Secondly, what are we to make of the comments in...I believe it was Episode 3ish but they do say it more than once...that the human birth rate has been mysteriously declining ever since Second Impact? They don't really focus on this at all, but apparently the birth rate is dropping and they have no idea why. That is, obviously the result of ecological disaster and global wars has made it drop a little, but its dropped off far more than can be explained by these physical reasons. The best I could come up with is that this is an OUTRIGHT theological reference to the fact that the "End Times" are approaching..... ....however, all of the theological stuff on the show usually has a pseudo-scientific explanation of sorts, and moreover...the birthrate hasn't dropped off entirely, its just less than it used to be. So....why? Or was this a plotline later abandoned?

[]:             Quote Post by thewayneiac » Mon Mar 17, 2008 7:33 pm Ritsuko wrote: "The Evas do not intrinsically have souls, but human souls reside within them. They were all salvaged. The only vessel that contained a soul was Rei. She was the only one born with a soul. The Chamber of Guf had been emptied. The Rei-like things here have no souls. They're just vessels."

[]:             Quote Post by V » Mon Mar 17, 2008 8:03 pm is that Lilith needs to be in the Black Moon undisturbed to issue Lilim souls properly.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Mar 17, 2008 9:51 pm He doesn't seem to think so (anymore).

[]:             Quote Post by Semisubtle » Tue Mar 18, 2008 4:22 pm thewayneiac wrote: The one thing I've always been certain of, though I always get some argument about it here, is that she is saying that the Evas have salvaged souls because the Chamber of Guf is empty. That is, they were originally planning to use unborn souls from the Chamber in the Evas, but "the cupboard was bare", so to speak, forcing them to use salvaged souls. I can't see any other interpretation of these lines that make sense.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Tue Mar 18, 2008 4:54 pm I always saw it as "We can't make souls, it's like the Chamber of Guf is functionally empty, so we gotta borrow them from other people." As for the declining birth rate, I figured that was Adam's Anti-AT Field during Second Impact really messing crap up, by managing to, atleast partly, reboot the ecosystem. So now whatever esoteric NGE process that imbues life with souls and ensures for safe birth delivery in Lilith-derived ecosystems is "missing lines of code" and having compile errors, to use a geeky analogy.

[]:             Quote Post by Steve » Sun Mar 30, 2008 6:36 am I think I'd have to agree with V's second theory. I don't think Ritsuko meant that there are no more souls altogether, because as has been mentioned that would just raise more questions about the world post-Third Impact and the fact that every child born after the point of "emptying" would have no soul. In context it seems to just be a way of describing the fact that they couldn't create new souls themselves. I wonder, if indeed she meant it more literally - there are no more souls - how would she/NERV have known this? I don't have any ideas on this, short of it being in the Dead Sea Scrolls or something.

[]: Revelatory Thought             Quote Post by henna » Sun Aug 16, 2009 10:36 am Ritsuko's line about the Chamber of Guf being empty actually makes a surprising amount of sense, given the way humanity works. Given that human life springs from the blood of Lillith, along with the first human souls, and the Doors of Guf on Lillith's palms resemble vulva, would it not therefore make sense that after the first outpouring of souls from Lillith, any additional souls are actually created by the mother while the child is in growing? Adam based life doesn't seem to reproduce (it wouldn't need to), so this theory, coupled with the possibility of reincarnation would explain why 'new' humans are born.

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Sun Aug 16, 2009 11:06 am ....wat no that doesn't make sense as all souls come from Chamber of Guf. Chamber of Guf being Black Moon & Lilith's body Vaginas and shit are symbolic

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Sun Aug 16, 2009 2:30 pm Considering that it's probable that souls come from some quantum plane of existence, Lilith's Chamber of Gulf at best acted like a doorway for the initial souls to enter her original lifeforms. It's emptiness could be a result of the Seed not being able to channel new souls without it's own resident soul?

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Sun Aug 16, 2009 3:18 pm AuraTwilight wrote: Considering that it's probable that souls come from some quantum plane of existence, Lilith's Chamber of Gulf at best acted like a doorway for the initial souls to enter her original lifeforms. It's emptiness could be a result of the Seed not being able to channel new souls without it's own resident soul?

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Aug 16, 2009 6:15 pm Xard wrote: just because souls operate on quantum level doesn't mean they're quantum. All in show and CI indicates souls just come out of great Mother being

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Sun Aug 16, 2009 7:11 pm I prefer the notion that Lilith just heaved out an amount of soul-stuff and that individual souls are constructed from this material in concert with biological processes.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Mon Aug 17, 2009 7:31 am As for the diminishing number of souls thing I think it's Anno's take on Japan's lowering birthrates which were just getting worse and worse in 90s IIRC - I don't think there's much instory rationale for it

[]:             Quote Post by Teague » Mon Aug 17, 2009 7:48 am Perhaps...perhaps all the people that got tanged by Adam had their soul destroyed as well...and the sudden vacuum damaged the whole process...although...I can't image more than a hundred people or so were directly caught in 2I... ...Unlikely...but it would be interesting if Adam re-purposed the souls of the Antarctic expedition into the souls of the angels...if Longinus somehow messed up Adam's soul making abilities...or something...

[]:             Quote Post by Allemann » Mon Aug 17, 2009 3:35 pm Is the Chamber Of Guf in Lilith inside or outside of her?

[]:             Quote Post by FallenTabris » Mon Aug 17, 2009 3:56 pm I don't know what the chamber of guf in reference to adam or lilith has to do with Rei (enough to be in ritsuko's Rei speech).

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Mon Aug 17, 2009 4:39 pm Reichu wrote: Xard wrote: just because souls operate on quantum level doesn't mean they're quantum. All in show and CI indicates souls just come out of great Mother being

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Tue Aug 18, 2009 3:42 pm ...Unlikely...but it would be interesting if Adam re-purposed the souls of the Antarctic expedition into the souls of the angels...if Longinus somehow messed up Adam's soul making abilities...or something...

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue Aug 18, 2009 6:40 pm Lilith's Chamber of Guf is somehow inside her and the Black Moon at the same time. Adam had one of her own. Presumably every Seed comes with one. AuraTwilight wrote: Unlikely, or else we'd probably of had an Angel that thought he was Dr. Katsuragi, and attacked the area looking for his daughter. Which would cue for an extremely kickass Misato-contamination scene.

The "Chamber of Guf is empty" is just a "fancy way" of saying "we can't make souls on our own from scratch with clones, and we couldn't get new ones from Adam because Adam was dormant. So we jerry-rigged the system by either using transfered human souls, or fractions of human souls we found" etc. What was everyone else's position? Secondly, what are we to make of the comments in...I believe it was Episode 3ish but they do say it more than once...that the human birth rate has been mysteriously declining ever since Second Impact?
